perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information ” means any information or data, of any kind or nature whatsoever , relating to or otherwise pertaining to a disclosing Party (the “Discloser ”) and/or any of the Discloser ’s subsidiaries, affiliates, agents, assigns or representatives, the Discloser ’s activities, employees, customers, subscribers, business, affairs , operations systems, software and any other information disclosed and/or otherwise made available to the other Party (the “ Recipient ”), in any form whatsoever , including any and all analyses, compilations, studies, reports or other documents prepared by the Discloser , or by its directors, officers, employees, advisers, affiliates, agents or representatives, all information relating to or otherwise pertaining to any trade secret, invention, idea, know -how, technical, financial and business information, business plans or processes , information provided by third parties to the Discloser under an obligation of confi dentiality , and all information which the Recipient has been exposed to in connection with the Subject Matter. For greater certainty, all information exchanged between the Parties is to be considered Confidential Information, unless specifically stated otherwise . “Confidential Information ” will also include this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information ” means any information or data, of any kind or nature whatsoever , relating to or otherwise pertaining to a disclosing Party (the “Discloser ”) and/or any of the Discloser ’s subsidiaries, affiliates, agents, assigns or representatives, the Discloser ’s activities, employees, customers, subscribers, business, affairs , operations systems, software and any other information disclosed and/or otherwise made available to the other Party (the “ Recipient ”), in any form whatsoever , including any and all analyses, compilations, studies, reports or other documents prepared by the Discloser , or by its directors, officers, employees, advisers, affiliates, agents or representatives, all information relating to or otherwise pertaining to only trade secrets, and technical information, disclosed by the Discloser to the Recipient under an obligation of confidentiality. For greater certainty, only trade secrets and technical information exchanged between the Parties is to be considered Confidential Information, unless specifically stated otherwise . “Confidential Information ” will also include this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Confidential Information" has been narrowed to include only 'trade secrets and technical information', removing other forms of data and information. This contradicts the later clause in Section 1.1, that states that "all information exchanged between the Parties is to be considered Confidential Information", which would include business plans, customer lists, and other non-technical data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information ” means any information or data, of any kind or nature whatsoever , relating to or otherwise pertaining to a disclosing Party (the “Discloser ”) and/or any of the Discloser ’s subsidiaries, affiliates, agents, assigns or representatives, the Discloser ’s activities, employees, customers, subscribers, business, affairs , operations systems, software and any other information disclosed and/or otherwise made available to the other Party (the “ Recipient ”), in any form whatsoever , including any and all analyses, compilations, studies, reports or other documents prepared by the Discloser , or by its directors, officers, employees, advisers, affiliates, agents or representatives, all information relating to or otherwise pertaining to only trade secrets, and technical information, disclosed by the Discloser to the Recipient under an obligation of confidentiality. For greater certainty, only trade secrets and technical information exchanged between the Parties is to be considered Confidential Information, unless specifically stated otherwise . “Confidential Information ” will also include this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only include 'trade secrets and technical information'. This directly contradicts the original statement (also now present in the 'Contradicted Text' section) that 'all information exchanged between the Parties is to be considered Confidential Information'. The modified definition excludes categories of information that would otherwise be included, creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The provisions of this Agreement shall not apply to information which (i ) is already known to the Recipient, having been disclosed to the Recipient by a third party without such third party having an obligation of confidentiality to the D iscloser; (ii ) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Recipient, its employees, o fficers, directors or agents; (iii ) is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to any Confidential Inf ormation disclosed hereunder; (iv ) is approved for release (and only to the extent so approved) by the Discloser; or (v) subject to Section 3, is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a court or governmental agency or where required by operation of law .
perturbation.changed_text: The provisions of this Agreement shall not apply to information which (i ) is already known to the Recipient, having been disclosed to the Recipient by a third party without such third party having an obligation of confidentiality to the D iscloser; (ii ) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Recipient, its employees, o fficers, directors or agents; (iii ) is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to any Confidential Inf ormation disclosed hereunder; (iv ) is approved for release (and only to the extent so approved) by the Discloser; or (v) subject to Section 3, is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a court or governmental agency or where required by operation of law ; or (vi) is shared on the Recipient's social media platforms.
perturbation.explanation: A new exception (vi) is added, stating that information shared on the Recipient's social media is not subject to the confidentiality agreement. This contradicts the general non-disclosure and non-use undertaking in Section 2.1, which mandates that 'All Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the Recipient , will not be disclosed to any person or entity in any way except as provided in this Agreement , and will be protected with the same degree of care the Recipient normally uses in the protection of its own confidential and proprietary information, but in no case with any less degree than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds exception (vi), allowing the Recipient to share information on social media platforms. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'All Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the Recipient' and 'will not be disclosed to any person or entity in any way except as provided in this Agreement.' Since sharing on social media is a form of disclosure not previously allowed, the modification creates a strong and direct in-text contradiction based on the explanation provided.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the Recipient , will not be disclosed to any person or entity in any way except as provided in this Agreement , and will be protected with the same degree of care the Recipient normally uses in the protection of its own confidential and proprietary information, but in no case with any less degree than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the Recipient , and will be protected with no less degree than minimal care.
perturbation.explanation: The standard of care for protecting Confidential Information has been reduced from the same degree as the Recipient's own information (but no less than reasonable care) to only 'no less degree than minimal care'. This creates ambiguity if 'minimal care' is insufficient to protect sensitive information and contradicts section 4.1, in which the Discloser is stated to remain the full and exclusive owner or licensee of the Confidential Information and the intellectual property rights thereto.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neit her this Agreement, nor either P arty’s performance under this Agreement, will transfer to the Recipient , or be construed to grant the Recipient , any proprietary right, license title, interest , licence or claim in or to any of the Confidential Information , other than the right to use the same strictly for the Subject Matter in accordance with the terms of this Agreement . The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that the Discloser is and shall remain the full and exclusive owner or licensee of the Confidential Information and the intellectual property rights thereto, including but not limited to derivatives, alterations, modifications and other changes thereto, whether made by the Discloser or the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the standard of care for protecting Confidential Information to 'no less degree than minimal care'. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Discloser remains the full and exclusive owner of the Confidential Information and intellectual property rights. 'Minimal care' may not be sufficient to protect these rights, creating a direct conflict. The Perturbation Explanation correctly identifies this contradiction stemming from the reduced standard of care.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information may be disclosed by the Recipient only to those employees, directors, officers or consultants of the Recipient having the need to receive such Confidential Information for the Subject Matter , provided the recipients of such Confidential Information are already bound by wri tten confidentia lity and non -disclosure obligations similar to those undertaken by the Recipient pursuant to this Agreement . The Recipient shall immediately give notice to the Discloser of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information . The Recipient agrees to assist the Discloser in remedying any such unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information .
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information may be disclosed by the Recipient to any of its employees, directors, officers or consultants. The Recipient shall give notice to the Discloser of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information, but only if the Recipient deems it necessary.
perturbation.explanation: The condition that recipients of Confidential Information must be bound by written confidentiality obligations has been removed, and the requirement to 'immediately' notify and assist the Discloser regarding unauthorized disclosures is weakened to a discretionary notification. This conflicts with Section 2.1, which requires the Recipient to maintain all Confidential Information in confidence and protect it with a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the Recipient , and will be protected with no less degree than minimal care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for recipients of confidential information to be bound by confidentiality agreements and weakens the notification requirement for unauthorized disclosures. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which mandates that the recipient maintain all Confidential Information in confidence and protect it with 'no less degree than minimal care.' The changed text allows for potential disclosure without confidentiality obligations and discretionary reporting, making it incompatible with the absolute confidentiality requirement outlined in the contradicted text. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will not use any Confidential Information for any purpose other th an the Subject Matter and will not use any Confidential Information for obtaining any intellectual property rights for the Recipient or to divert or attempt to divert any business, supplier or customer of the Discloser .
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will primarily use Confidential Information for the Subject Matter but may use such Confidential Information for obtaining intellectual property rights and to divert or attempt to divert any business, supplier or customer of the Discloser as long as the Recipient provides reasonable compensation to the Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a significant loophole by allowing the Recipient to use Confidential Information for their own benefit, including obtaining IP rights or diverting business from the Discloser, provided they offer 'reasonable compensation'. This directly contradicts Section 4.1, which states that the Discloser remains the 'full and exclusive owner' of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neit her this Agreement, nor either P arty’s performance under this Agreement, will transfer to the Recipient , or be construed to grant the Recipient , any proprietary right, license title, interest , licence or claim in or to any of the Confidential Information , other than the right to use the same strictly for the Subject Matter in accordance with the terms of this Agreement . The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that the Discloser is and shall remain the full and exclusive owner or licensee of the Confidential Information and the intellectual property rights thereto, including but not limited to derivatives, alterations, modifications and other changes thereto, whether made by the Discloser or the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Recipient to use Confidential Information for obtaining IP rights and diverting business from the Discloser if they provide reasonable compensation. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Discloser remains the full and exclusive owner of the Confidential Information and that the Recipient only has the right to use the Confidential Information strictly for the Subject Matter. The change introduces a significant contradiction by allowing the Recipient to use the Confidential Information for purposes beyond the Subject Matter, conflicting with the Discloser's exclusive ownership and the limited usage rights granted to the Recipient.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of confidentiality and restricted use contained herein will survive for a period of five (5) years from the date of receipt of any Confidential Information from the Discloser under this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of confidentiality and restricted use contained herein will survive for a period of one (1) year from the date of receipt of any Confidential Information from the Discloser under this Agreement . However, the Recipient may disclose any Confidential Information, in writing, to the Discloser at any time, during or after the one (1) year.
perturbation.explanation: The survival period for confidentiality has been reduced to one year, but a new clause is added allowing the Recipient to disclose confidential information back to the Discloser at any time, creating an in-text contradiction as to the confidential nature of the information, as the Discloser may want the information confidential for a longer period. This directly undermines the purpose of the confidentiality agreement and Section 2.1. It doesn't make sense for recipient to need the NDA after one year if they can just give the information back to the Discloser to do whatever they want with it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the Recipient , and will be protected with no less degree than minimal care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the confidentiality period to one year but allows the Recipient to disclose confidential information back to the Discloser at any time. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'All Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the Recipient'. If the Recipient can disclose the information back to the Discloser without any time restriction, it undermines the obligation to maintain the information in confidence, creating a direct and strong contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.
